Operation: 1. Center and chuck in lathe. 2. Turn OD to 0.748". 3. Drill bore 0.472" through full length. 4. Part at final length + 0.25" for end-facing.
Tooling: Lathe; 0.472" Forstner or D-bit; OD turning tools
Notes / EMPIRICAL flags: Body length = 13.25" EMPIRICAL — add 0.50" stock for tuning trim. Final length after reed fit and tuning check.

Finished Dimensions: 0.472" bore extended ~0.50" at reed end (no change in diameter for simple socket)
Operation: Socket is simply the upper bore opening — reed shank + PTFE tape seals it. No machined socket step required for first build.
Notes / EMPIRICAL flags: Simple friction-fit with PTFE wrap. Stepped socket is an upgrade option.

Operation: Mark centers per hole-schedule drawing (EMPIRICAL positions). Drill with brad-point bit. Start with 0.1875" pilot; open to 0.25". Undercut if response is flat.
Tooling: Drill press; brad-point bits 0.1875"" + 0.25""; V-block jig
Notes / EMPIRICAL flags: Hole diameter EMPIRICAL. Test intonation with reed before committing. Undercutting widens effective dia without enlarging surface. See jig-decision.md.
